content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from Topic A's ICM-based autonomous agents) can be repurposed to prioritize what new data and past experiences get stored in long-term memory for state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agents, creating a self-organizing memory system that automatically prunes low-value information while retaining high-uncertainty, knowledge-expanding content. This removes the need for hand-engineered memory retention rules that currently limit active learning agent scalability.
